title: Xyroschoenus hornei
taxonomy: Xyroschoenus hornei is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae, commonly known as the sedge family. This family comprises over 5,000 species of sedges, found in a wide range of habitats across the globe. Xyroschoenus hornei is further classified within the genus Xyroschoenus, which includes several other species of sedges. The genus Xyroschoenus is part of the tribe Cypereae, a group of sedges that are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Xyroschoenus hornei reflects its close evolutionary relationships with other sedge species within the Cyperaceae family.

intro: Xyloschoenus hornei, also known as Horn's Club Sedge or Horn's Xyloschoenus, is a perennial grass-like plant belonging to the family Cyperaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in relieving respiratory issues and skin infections. Bioactive constituents found in Xyloschoenus hornei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, the plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
